zotero.abstractNote: Cooperative transport of a payload by multiple mobile manipulators is a complex task due to the high dimensionality of the state space and the non-holonomic constraints of the mobile bases. In this paper, we propose a motion planning framework for a team of mobile manipulators transporting a common payload. We model the system as a single kinematic chain with a closed-loop structure. We use a sampling-based motion planner to find a collision-free path for the payload in the workspace. The motion of the mobile bases and the manipulators is then derived using the inverse kinematics of the closed-loop system. We validate our approach through simulations and experiments with a team of two mobile manipulators.
zotero.conferenceName: 15th IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering
zotero.proceedingsTitle: 15th IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering
